text: Last class you demoed the platform, linking each feature to a result.
Today you turn structure, data and the demo into one investor pitch.

First, the problem; then, our solution.
Revenue has grown sharply, and churn has fallen.
The whole process is handled in one place.
That is a fair question. Let me come back to it with data.
First, the problem. Renting a home is slow and full of paperwork. Our platform fixes that, and the whole process is handled in one place.
Good opening. But why should I believe this will work? Show me the numbers.
Revenue has grown sharply, and churn has fallen to a record low. We have strong traction in three new cities.
And what is your burn rate? A great product with no runway is still a risk.
That is a fair question. Our burn rate is low, so our runway is long. We are raising capital to scale faster, in return for equity.
Now that is a complete pitch. Send me the term sheet, and let us talk valuation.
We have strong traction in three new cities.
We are raising capital to scale faster, in return for equity.

Good afternoon. Today I want to walk you through why Quinto Andar is the partner you have been waiting for. First, the problem. Renting a home is still slow and full of paperwork. Then, our solution. Our platform is fully integrated, so the whole process is handled in one place. Now the numbers. Revenue has grown sharply, and our churn has fallen to a record low, which means our customers stay. We have strong traction in three new cities. To sum up, the market is large, the product is proven, and the runway is clear. We are raising capital to scale faster, and this is the moment to come on board.

Let me think about how to pull this whole block together for the pitch. In lesson eleven I learned to structure a talk with signposting, so the investor never gets lost. In lesson twelve I learned to tell the story of the numbers, not just read a chart. In lesson thirteen I learned to stay calm in the Q&A and buy time instead of guessing. And in lesson fourteen I learned to demo the product in the passive, linking each feature to a result. A pitch is all four at once. I open with a clear structure, I bring the data alive, I show the product, and I am ready for the hardest question. If I can do that in ten minutes, the room is mine.

First, you open with the problem the investor already feels. Then you present your solution and signpost each part clearly. After that, you bring the numbers alive and show the traction behind them. Next, you demo the product and link each feature to a result. Finally, you make the ask and handle every question with confidence.

In return, we offer ten percent equity.
